revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:吾妻神社 (二宮町)]]


{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|名称 = {{ruby|Azuma Shrine|あづまじんじゃ|qq=}}

|所在地 = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Naka District, Kanagawa|en|Naka District, Kanagawa|qid=Q1202849|lt=Naka District|1=Naka District, Kanagawa}}{{ill|Ninomiya, Kanagawa|en|Ninomiya, Kanagawa|qid=Q1354853|lt=Ninomiya Town|1=Ninomiya, Kanagawa}}, Yamashiro area
|緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 17 |緯度秒 = 53.9
|経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 15 |経度秒 = 1.4
|地図 = {{Location map|Kanagawa, Japan|lat_deg=35|lat_min=17|lat_sec=53.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=15|lon_sec=1.4|label={{ill|Azuma Shrine|ja|吾妻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuma Shrine|qq=}}|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|qq=}}
|祭神 = {{ill|Tachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|弟橘姫命|lt=Tachibana-hime|lt_ja=弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487396}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}
|社格 =
|創建 = Unknown
|本殿 =
|別名 =
|札所等 =
|例祭 =
|神事 =
|location = {{ill|Ninomiya|qid=Q1354853|1=Ninomiya, Kanagawa}}
|coordinates = {{Coord|35.298233|N|139.250363|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|deity = {{ill|Ototachibanahime|qid=Q11487396|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}}
}}
'''Azuma Shrine''' (吾妻神社, あづまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ninomiya, Kanagawa|en|Ninomiya, Kanagawa|qid=Q1354853|lt=Ninomiya Town|1=Ninomiya, Kanagawa}} in {{ill|Naka District, Kanagawa|en|Naka District, Kanagawa|qid=Q1202849|lt=Naka District|1=Naka District, Kanagawa}}, Japan, situated within {{ill|Azuma Mountain Park|ja|吾妻山公園|lt=Azuma Mountain|lt_ja=吾妻山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuma Mountain Park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吾妻山 (神奈川県)|1=Mount Azuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11416623}}.
== Deities ==
* {{ill|Tachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|弟橘姫命|lt=Tachibana-hime|lt_ja=弟橘媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487396}}
* {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}
== History ==
The exact founding date of this shrine is unknown; however, according to tradition, during Yamato Takeru’s eastern expedition he encountered a violent storm on the {{ill|Uraga Channel|en|Uraga Channel|qid=Q723431|lt=Uraga Waterway|1=Uraga Channel}}, and in order to calm the tempest, his consort, {{ill|Tachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja|弟橘姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弟橘媛|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487396}}, entered the water. Later, her comb washed ashore, and enshrining that comb led to the establishment of this shrine.<ref>{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 山西村 吾妻社|qq=}}</ref> Additionally, the shrine is mentioned in travelogues written by the Confucian scholar {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|en|Kaibara Ekken|qid=Q1721396|lt=Kaihara Ekken|1=Kaibara Ekken}} ({{ill|1630|en|1630|qid=Q6856|lt=1630 AD|1=1630}}–{{ill|1714|en|1714|qid=Q6878|lt=1714 AD|1=1714}}) during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo Period|1=Edo period}}.
In October {{ill|1935|en|1935|qid=Q18658|lt=1935 AD|1=1935}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}} 10th year), it was selected in a reader poll conducted by {{ill|Yokohama Trade Shinpō|ja|横浜貿易新報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Trade Shinpō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神奈川新聞|1=Kanagawa Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q840928}} as one of the {{ill|45 Best Scenic and Historic Sites of the Prefecture|ja|県下名勝史蹟四十五佳選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/45 Best Scenic and Historic Sites of the Prefecture|1=Q135267871|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q135267871}}.<ref name="市史通信">{{Cite journal|lang=ja|journal=市史通信|volume=6|title=1935th year 神奈川県名勝・史蹟投票|author=Toshio Momose|publisher=Yokohama City Historical Archives|year=2009|pages=8-10|qq=}}</ref>
== Location ==
* {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{ill|Naka District, Kanagawa|en|Naka District, Kanagawa|qid=Q1202849|lt=Naka District|1=Naka District, Kanagawa}} {{ill|Ninomiya, Kanagawa|en|Ninomiya, Kanagawa|qid=Q1354853|lt=Ninomiya Town|1=Ninomiya, Kanagawa}}, Yamashiro 1117
== Footnotes ==

{{Reflist|qq=}}
== References ==
* {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 37, 11 Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 2nd volume, Section of Tōayagun, Volume 2|chapter=Ninomiya Shō, Yamashiro Village, Azuma Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179210/151|qq=}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 山西村 吾妻社|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzan-kaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11659206}}|date=August 1932|qq=}}
